Belfast was rocked by fiery disorder into the night when hundreds of masked protestors took to the streets in response to Monday's brutal knife attack. A number of houses, cars and a bus were torched across the city, amid suggestions that non-white residents were deliberately targeted by some of the fires. Northern Ireland's First Minister, Michelle O'Neill, condemned the 'outright thuggery', saying that 'groups of masked men burning families out of their homes is nothing less than disgusting cowardice'.

I’ve been looking to downsize for the last two years. I wouldn’t touch a retirement home unless I had enough capital not to be concerned about service charges. My problem is the service charges on private flats. Coastal towns in the SE already suffer from inflated prices as Londoners move out and commute. But when you check out ground rent and service charges, then take out estate agent fees and moving costs, I’m far better off staying put and hiring a bit of help with gardening now and again.

The Queen's club in West London might be named after Queen Victoria, but there was only one queen in town on Tuesday evening - and only one Victoria, too. At the age of 44 Serena Williams played her first professional tennis match in four years, teaming up with Victoria Mboko - 25 years her junior - in the HSBC Championship doubles. And, against all reasonable expectations, they won. They won by 7-6, 6-2 against No3 seeds Nicole Melichar-Martinez and Erin Routliffe, a pair of doubles specialists with three Grand Slam titles between them. 'I had so much fun,' said Serena on court. 'Vicky was really able to hold up the team on the big points. It felt so natural playing with her.'

It’s nearly comical to me how people who have never grieved the death of someone who was the biggest part of their lives, seem to think a couple weeks or a couple months “dulls the pain” or they’ll be moving forward. I was a funeral director for years before my father died and I was a mess for the first year. Then fine for 6 months into the 2nd year, then a mess for the next 6 months, and now moving forward (he died in October 2023). The thing that surprised me the most is how come NOBODY ever told me how losing the most important parent in your life changes the world and changes you and you will just never be the same person again. I’m 50.

If boots were to be listed I don’t think it would be a great investment in my opinion from a long time customer. Boots used to be a great retailer in its field but sadly in recent years has gone of the boil, the annoyance of using self service tills isn’t a great customer experience but it’s the lack of stock on the shelves especially when they do a two for three offer and then lack the stock in the back room, I have given up the amount of times I have walked out empty handed from either of two stores I try to use and end up ordering online when I want to purchase on the high street. Some staff are helpful while other haven’t a clue or just plain rude. I am just confused as to what a Boots store is supposed to actually be, a place to buy products or some where to visit on a Saturday afternoon and just shuffle about the perfume counter for something to do.

Boots' owner Sycamore Partners is in discussions with potential buyers including the family behind Primark as well as Australian pharmacists Sigma Healthcare.
  • 13 comments
  • share Boots set to snub stock market return as it eyes £7.5bn sale

Rarely-seen pop star Sia steps out makeup-free in Los Angeles

Fans are used to seeing her dressed up with her face covered - usually by a large bow. But on Sunday, Australian pop star Sia swapped her trademark dramatics for a low-key look as she visited a farmers' market in Los Angeles. The singer, 50, looked relaxed in a cream kaftan layered over a white T-shirt as she perused the stalls and picked up some bags of vegetables.
  • 234 comments
  • share Rarely-seen Australian pop star steps out makeup-free in Los Angeles

I'm not buying another machine.

An Italian coffee giant has launched an wild new product to make espresso, in a bid to challenge Keurig's K-Cup dominance.
  • 19 comments
  • share Coffee giant's radical new espresso machine to compete with Keurig

I don’t care if I get it hundreds of red arrows, but I think in today’s society women would probably die of exhaustion doing this kind of work and go into deep depression because they wouldn’t have so much time for themselves. I mean, they would miss out on their daily gym sessions. And by the way, I am a woman but of a different generation as you can see I’m proud of it. I also went to work.

The handbook, which would horrify feminists today, also suggests the best types of linoleum floorings for each room of the house.  A 'light coloured' lino was considered the ideal choice for a bedroom because it is 'cleaner, more cheerful looking' and does not 'show dust so easily'. In an era when a woman's place was considered to be in the home, the 'housewifery notes' complied by Margaret Pimlott, of Bowden, Cheshire, also include diagrams and instructions for cleaning lavatory drains, baking cakes and infant feeding. The 130-year-old notebook was found amongst family papers in a trunk in an attic at a Cheshire home by a descendant during a recent clear out.
  • 9 comments
  • share Housewife's Handbook: Victorian's 11-step guide to cleaning a bedroom
